label: 101 to 110 Repealed. –(11), (f)(2)–(4)(A), (g)(9), , , 2294Pub. L. 103–325, title VI, § 602(e)(5)108 Stat. 2292Sept. 23, 1994
notes: Section 101, acts , ; , , provided for delivery of circulating notes in blank to national banking associations depositing bonds with Treasurer of United States.Mar. 14, 1900, ch. 41, § 1231 Stat. 49Oct. 5, 1917, ch. 74, § 240 Stat. 342
Section 101a, R.S. § 5159; , ; , , related to deposit of bonds to secure circulating notes.Dec. 23, 1913, ch. 6, § 1738 Stat. 268June 21, 1917, ch. 32, § 940 Stat. 239
Section 102, R.S. § 5158, construed term “United States bonds” as including registered bonds.
Section 103, , , related to denominations of notes and limitation on amount of $1 and $2 notes.act Oct. 5, 1917, ch. 74, § 340 Stat. 342

Section 104, R.S. § 5172; , ; , ; , ; , , related to printing and form of circulating notes.May 30, 1908, ch. 229, § 1135 Stat. 551Dec. 23, 1913, ch. 6, § 2738 Stat. 274Aug. 4, 1914, ch. 22538 Stat. 682Mar. 3, 1919, ch. 101, § 440 Stat. 1315
Section 105, , , provided that Comptroller of Currency was to print charter numbers of association on national bank notes.act June 20, 1874, ch. 343, § 518 Stat. 124
Section 106, , , provided for printing national-bank notes on distinctive paper adopted by Secretary of the Treasury.act Mar. 3, 1875, ch. 130, § 118 Stat. 372
Section 107, R.S. § 5173, related to custody of plates and dies procured for printing notes and payment of expenses.

Section 108, R.S. § 5174; , , related to examination of plates, dies, and other material from which national-bank circulation was printed, and destruction of obsolete material.Feb. 27, 1877, ch. 69, § 119 Stat. 252

Section 109, R.S. § 5182; , , provided that banks could issue and circulate notes the same as money if signed by officers in manner of obligatory promissory notes payable on demand at place of business, and specified demands for which such notes were to be received.Jan. 13, 1920, ch. 3841 Stat. 387
Section 110, R.S. § 5183; , , prohibited banks from issuing unauthorized notes.Feb. 18, 1875, ch. 80, § 118 Stat. 320
